prompt: Generate a grouped bar chart showing stock levels of three products (Image 1–3) across four regions. Use each product image to label bars on the x-axis and color each bar to reflect its packaging.
prompt_refined: Generate a grouped bar chart that visually represents the stock levels of three distinct products across four different geographical regions. On the X-axis, use image 1, image 2, and image 3 as the visual labels for each product group. Each bar within a product group must be colored to reflect the primary hues of its corresponding packaging: bars for image 1 should be predominantly yellow and dark green; bars for image 2 should be primarily yellow with red and blue accents; and bars for image 3 should be predominantly white with orange and green accents. The Y-axis of the chart should be clearly labeled 'Stock Levels'.
objects: Product group 1 visual label (Nature Valley Oats'n Honey Granola Bars box)
Product group 2 visual label (Nesquik cereal box)
Product group 3 visual label (Tropicana Orange Juice carton)
Bars for product group 1 (yellow and dark green)
Bars for product group 2 (yellow with red and blue accents)
Bars for product group 3 (white with orange and green accents)
